text.NABRE: 9:16 Ephraim is stricken, their root is dried up; they will bear no fruit. Were they to bear children, I would slay the beloved of their womb.

text.NRSV: My God will reject them because they have not listened to him; they shall be wanderers among the nations.
text.Rotherham: My God will reject them, because they hearkened not unto him,—that they may become wanderers throughout the nations.
text.KJV: 9:17 My God will cast them away, because they did not hearken vnto him: and they shalbe wanderers among the nations.
text.NABRE: 9:17 My God will disown them because they have not listened to him; they will be wanderers among the nations.

text.NRSV: Israel is a luxuriant vine that yields its fruit. The more his fruit increased the more altars he built; as his land improved he erected more beautiful pillars.
text.Rotherham: A luxuriant vine, is Israel, fruit, beseemeth him,—According to the abundance of his fruit, hath he brought abundance to the altars, according to the goodliness of his land, hath he made goodly statues.
text.KJV: 10:1 Israel is an empty vine, he bringeth forth fruite vnto himselfe: according to the multitude of his fruite, he hath increased the altars, according to the goodnesse of his land, they haue made goodly images.
text.NABRE: 10:1 Chapter 10 Destruction of Idolatrous Cultic Objects - Israel is a luxuriant vine whose fruit matches its growth. The more abundant his fruit, the more altars he built; The more productive his land, the more sacred pillars he set up.

text.NRSV: Their heart is false; now they must bear their guilt. The Lord will break down their altars, and destroy their pillars.
text.Rotherham: Hypocritical is their heart, Now, shall they be held guilty,—He, will break down their altars, he will destroy their statutes.
text.KJV: 10:2 Their heart is diuided: now shall they be found faultie: hee shall breake downe their altars: he shall spoile their images.
text.NABRE: 10:2 Their heart is false! Now they will pay for their guilt: God will break down their altars and destroy their sacred pillars.

text.NRSV: For now they will say: “We have no king, for we do not fear the Lord, and a king—what could he do for us?”
text.Rotherham: For, now, will they say, We have no king,—for we revere not Yahweh, and what could, a king, do for us?
text.KJV: 10:3 For now they shall say, We haue no King, because we feared not the Lord, What then should a King doe to vs?
text.NABRE: 10:3 For now they will say, “We have no king! Since we do not fear the LORD, the king—what could he do for us?”

text.NRSV: They utter mere words; with empty oaths they make covenants; so litigation springs up like poisonous weeds in the furrows of the field.
text.Rotherham: They have spoken words, swearing falsely, in solemnizing a covenant,—therefore shall judgment, spring up like a poisonous plant, on the ridges of the field.
text.KJV: 10:4 They haue spoken words, swearing falsely in making a couenant: thus iudgement springeth vp as hemlocke in the furrowes of the field.
text.NABRE: 10:4 They make promises, swear false oaths, and make covenants, While lawsuits sprout like poisonous weeds in the furrows of a field!

text.NRSV: The inhabitants of Samaria tremble for the calf of Beth-aven. Its people shall mourn for it, and its idolatrous priests shall wail over it, over its glory that has departed from it.
text.Rotherham: About the calves of Beth-aven, will the inhabitant of Samaria be concerned,—for the people thereof, have mourned over it, and, the ascetics thereof, who, over it, used to exult, [shall mourn] for the glory thereof, because it hath departed therefrom.
text.KJV: 10:5 The inhabitants of Samaria shall feare, because of the calues of Bethauen: for the people thereof shall mourne ouer it, and the priests thereof that reioyced on it, for the glory thereof, because it is departed from it.
text.NABRE: 10:5 The inhabitants of Samaria are afraid for the calf of Beth-aven; Its people mourn for it and its idolatrous priests wail over it, —over its glory which has departed from it.

text.NRSV: The thing itself shall be carried to Assyria as tribute to the great king. Ephraim shall be put to shame, and Israel shall be ashamed of his idol.
text.Rotherham: Itself also, to Assyria, shall be borne along, as a present to a hostile king,—shame, shall Ephraim receive, that Israel, may be ashamed, of his own counsel.
text.KJV: 10:6 It shall be also caried vnto Assyria for a present to King Iareb: Ephraim shall receiue shame, and Israel shall be ashamed of his owne counsell.
text.NABRE: 10:6 It too will be carried to Assyria, as an offering to the great king. Ephraim will be put to shame, Israel will be shamed by his schemes.

text.NRSV: Samaria’s king shall perish like a chip on the face of the waters.
text.Rotherham: Silenced is Samaria: her king, is as a chip on the face of the waters,
text.KJV: 10:7 As for Samaria, her King is cut off as the fome vpon the water.
text.NABRE: 10:7 Samaria and her king will disappear, like a twig upon the waters.

text.NRSV: The high places of Aven, the sin of Israel, shall be destroyed. Thorn and thistle shall grow up on their altars. They shall say to the mountains, “Cover us,” and to the hills, “Fall on us.”
text.Rotherham: So shall the high places of Aven, be destroyed, the sin of Israel, Thorn and prickle, shall come up on their altars,—Therefore shall they say to the mountains, Cover us, and to the hills, Fall on us.
text.KJV: 10:8 The high places also of Auen, the sinne of Israel, shall be destroyed: the thorne and the thistle shall come vp on their altars; and they shall say to the mountaines, Couer vs; and to the hilles, Fall on vs.
text.NABRE: 10:8 The high places of Aven will be destroyed, the sin of Israel; thorns and thistles will overgrow their altars. Then they will cry out to the mountains, “Cover us!” and to the hills, “Fall upon us!”

text.NRSV: Since the days of Gibeah you have sinned, O Israel; there they have continued. Shall not war overtake them in Gibeah?
text.Rotherham: Beyond the days of Gibeah, hast thou sinned, O Israel: there, came they to a stand, the battle against the sons of perversity, touched them not in Gibeah.
text.KJV: 10:9 O Israel, thou hast sinned from the dayes of Gibeah: there they stood: the battell in Gibeah against the children of iniquitie did not ouertake them.
text.NABRE: 10:9 War Because of Israel’s Wickedness - Since the days of Gibeah you have sinned, Israel. There they took their stand; will war not reach them in Gibeah? Against a perverse people

text.NRSV: I will come against the wayward people to punish them; and nations shall be gathered against them when they are punished for their double iniquity.
text.Rotherham: When I please, then will I chastise them,—and there shall be gathered together against them—peoples, they being harnessed to their two Iniquities;
text.KJV: 10:10 It is in my desire that I should chastise them, and the people shall be gathered against them, when they shall bind themselues in their two furrowes.
text.NABRE: 10:10 I came and I chastised them; Peoples will be gathered against them when I bind them to their two crimes.

text.NRSV: Ephraim was a trained heifer that loved to thresh, and I spared her fair neck; but I will make Ephraim break the ground; Judah must plow; Jacob must harrow for himself.
text.Rotherham: But, Ephraim, shall be a heifer broken in, loving to tread out corn, when, I, have passed over upon her fair neck,—I will drive Ephraim, Judah, shall plow, Jacob, shall harrow to him.
text.KJV: 10:11 And Ephraim is as an heifer that is taught and loueth to tread out the corne, but I passed ouer vpon her faire necke: I will make Ephraim to ride: Iudah shall plow, and Iacob shall breake his clods.
text.NABRE: 10:11 Ephraim was a trained heifer, that loved to thresh; I myself laid a yoke upon her beautiful neck; I will make Ephraim break ground, Judah must plow, Jacob must harrow for himself:

text.NRSV: Sow for yourselves righteousness; reap steadfast love; break up your fallow ground; for it is time to seek the Lord, that he may come and rain righteousness upon you.
text.Rotherham: Sow to yourselves in righteousness, reap ye at the bidding of lovingkindness, furrow to yourselves the newly-ploughed soil,—then will be the time to seek Yahweh, until he come, that he may rain down righteousness for you.
text.KJV: 10:12 Sow to your selues in righteousnesse, reape in mercie: breake vp your fallow ground: for it is time to seeke the Lord, till he come and raine righteousnesse vpon you.
text.NABRE: 10:12 “Sow for yourselves justice, reap the reward of loyalty; Break up for yourselves a new field, for it is time to seek the LORD, till he comes and rains justice upon you.”

text.NRSV: You have plowed wickedness, you have reaped injustice, you have eaten the fruit of lies. Because you have trusted in your power and in the multitude of your warriors,
text.Rotherham: Ye have plowed lawlessness, perversity, have ye reaped, ye have eaten the fruit of deception,—because thou didst trust in thy chariots, in the multitude of thy mighty men.
text.KJV: 10:13 Ye haue plowed wickednesse, yee haue reaped iniquitie, ye haue eaten the fruite of lies: because thou didst trust in thy way, in the multitude of thy mightie men.
text.NABRE: 10:13 But you have plowed wickedness, reaped perversity, and eaten the fruit of falsehood. Because you have trusted in your own power, and in your many warriors,

text.NRSV: therefore the tumult of war shall rise against your people, and all your fortresses shall be destroyed, as Shalman destroyed Beth-arbel on the day of battle when mothers were dashed in pieces with their children.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall there arise a tumult among thy peoples, and, all thy fortresses, shall be plundered, as Shalman plundered Beth-arbel, in the day of battle,—the mother, upon her children, dashed to the ground.
text.KJV: 10:14 Therefore shall a tumult arise among thy people, and all thy fortresses shall bee spoiled, as Shalman spoiled Beth-arbel in the day of battell: the mother was dashed in pieces vpon her children.
text.NABRE: 10:14 The clamor of war shall break out among your people and all your fortresses shall be ravaged As Salman ravaged Beth-arbel on the day of war, smashing mothers along with their children.

text.NRSV: Thus it shall be done to you, O Bethel, because of your great wickedness. At dawn the king of Israel shall be utterly cut off.
text.Rotherham: Even so, hath Bethel, done to you, because of your exceeding wickedness,—in the dawn, shall the king of Israel be, utterly silenced.
text.KJV: 10:15 So shall Bethel doe vnto you, because of your great wickednesse: in a morning shall the king of Israel be vtterly cut off.
text.NABRE: 10:15 So it will be done to you, Bethel, because of your utter wickedness: At dawn the king of Israel will utterly disappear.

text.NRSV: When Israel was a child, I loved him, and out of Egypt I called my son.
text.Rotherham: When Israel was, a child, then I loved him,—and, out of Egypt, called I my son.
text.KJV: 11:1 When Israel was a childe, then I loued him, and called my sonne out of Egypt.
text.NABRE: 11:1 Chapter 11 The Disappointment of a Parent - When Israel was a child I loved him, out of Egypt I called my son.

text.NRSV: The more I called them, the more they went from me; they kept sacrificing to the Baals, and offering incense to idols.
text.Rotherham: They invited them,—at once, they departed from before me, they, to the Baals, sacrificed, and, to the images, offered incense.
text.KJV: 11:2 As they called them, so they went from them: they sacrificed vnto Baalim, and burnt incense to grauen images.
text.NABRE: 11:2 The more I called them, the farther they went from me, Sacrificing to the Baals and burning incense to idols.

text.NRSV: Yet it was I who taught Ephraim to walk, I took them up in my arms; but they did not know that I healed them.
text.Rotherham: Yet, I, had taught Ephraim to walk, I used to take them upon mine arms,—But they acknowledged not that I had healed them.
text.KJV: 11:3 I taught Ephraim also to goe, taking them by their armes: but they knew not that I healed them.
text.NABRE: 11:3 Yet it was I who taught Ephraim to walk, who took them in my arms; but they did not know that I cared for them.

text.NRSV: I led them with cords of human kindness, with bands of love. I was to them like those who lift infants to their cheeks. I bent down to them and fed them.
text.Rotherham: With human cords, used I to draw them, with the bands of love, so became I unto them like those who remove the yoke [that was] on their jaws,—and, holding out [food] to him, I let him eat.
text.KJV: 11:4 I drew them with cords of a man, with bands of loue, and I was to them as they that take off the yoke on their iawes, and I laid meat vnto them.
text.NABRE: 11:4 I drew them with human cords, with bands of love; I fostered them like those who raise an infant to their cheeks; I bent down to feed them.

text.NRSV: They shall return to the land of Egypt, and Assyria shall be their king, because they have refused to return to me.
text.Rotherham: He was not to turn back into the land of Egypt, Howbeit, the Assyrian—he, became his king, for they refused to turn.
text.KJV: 11:5 ¶ He shall not returne into the land of Egypt; but the Assyrian shall be his king, because they refused to returne
text.NABRE: 11:5 He shall return to the land of Egypt, Assyria shall be his king, because they have refused to repent.

text.NRSV: The sword rages in their cities, it consumes their oracle-staves, and devours because of their schemes.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall the sword, rage, in his cities, and make an end of his multitudes, and consume them,—because of their counsels.
text.KJV: 11:6 And the sword shall abide on his cities, and shall consume his branches, and deuoure them, because of their own counsels.
text.NABRE: 11:6 The sword shall rage in his cities: it shall destroy his diviners, and devour them because of their schemings.

text.NRSV: My people are bent on turning away from me. To the Most High they call, but he does not raise them up at all.
text.Rotherham: But, my people, are bent towards turning from me, though upwards they call them, none of them can lift them.
text.KJV: 11:7 And my people are bent to backesliding from mee: though they called them to the most High, none at all would exalt him.
text.NABRE: 11:7 My people have their mind set on apostasy; though they call on God in unison, he shall not raise them up.

text.NRSV: How can I give you up, Ephraim? How can I hand you over, O Israel? How can I make you like Admah? How can I treat you like Zeboiim? My heart recoils within me; my compassion grows warm and tender.
text.Rotherham: How can I give thee up, Ephraim? abandon thee Israel? How can I make thee as Admah? set thee as Zeboim? Mine own heart, turneth against me, at once, are kindled my compassions.
text.KJV: 11:8 How shall I giue thee vp, Ephraim? how shall I deliuer thee, Israel? how shall I make thee as Admah? how shall I set thee as Zeboim? mine heart is turned within mee, my repentings are kindled together.
text.NABRE: 11:8 But Love Is Stronger and Restores - How could I give you up, Ephraim, or deliver you up, Israel? How could I treat you as Admah, or make you like Zeboiim? My heart is overwhelmed, my pity is stirred.

text.NRSV: I will not execute my fierce anger; I will not again destroy Ephraim; for I am God and no mortal, the Holy One in your midst, and I will not come in wrath.
text.Rotherham: I cannot execute the glow of mine anger, I cannot turn to destroy Ephraim,—for, GOD, am, I, and not man, When thou drawest near, [I am] a Holy One, though I do not enter a city.
text.KJV: 11:9 I will not execute the fiercenes of mine anger, I will not returne to destroy Ephraim, for I am God, and not man, the Holy One in the midst of thee, and I will not enter into the citie.
text.NABRE: 11:9 I will not give vent to my blazing anger, I will not destroy Ephraim again; For I am God and not a man, the Holy One present among you; I will not come in wrath.

text.NRSV: They shall go after the Lord, who roars like a lion; when he roars, his children shall come trembling from the west.
text.Rotherham: After Yahweh, let them go, Like a lion, will he roar,—When, he, shall roar, then let sons, come trembling, out of the West.
text.KJV: 11:10 They shal walke after the Lord: he shall roare like a lyon: when he shall roare, then the children shall tremble from the West.
text.NABRE: 11:10 They shall follow the LORD, who roars like a lion; When he roars, his children shall come frightened from the west,

text.NRSV: They shall come trembling like birds from Egypt, and like doves from the land of Assyria; and I will return them to their homes, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Let them come trembling like a small bird out of Egypt, and like a dove out of the land of Assyria,—so will I cause them to dwell by their own houses, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 11:11 They shall tremble as a bird out of Egypt, and as a doue out of the land of Assyria: and I will place them in their houses, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: 11:11 Out of Egypt they shall come trembling, like birds, like doves, from the land of Assyria; And I will resettle them in their homes, oracle of the LORD.

text.NRSV: Ephraim has surrounded me with lies, and the house of Israel with deceit; but Judah still walks with God, and is faithful to the Holy One.
text.Rotherham: They have compassed me about—with denial, Ephraim, with deceit, the house of Israel,—but, Judah, hath, again and again, run riot with GOD, though, with the holy places, entrusted.
text.KJV: 11:12 Ephraim compasseth mee about with lies, and the house of Israel with deceit: but Iudah yet ruleth with God, and is faithfull with the Saints.

text.NRSV: Ephraim herds the wind, and pursues the east wind all day long; they multiply falsehood and violence; they make a treaty with Assyria, and oil is carried to Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Ephraim, feedeth on wind, and pursueth the east wind, all the day, falsehood and force, doth he magnify,—and, a covenant with Assyria, would they solemnize, and, oil into Egypt, must be borne along.
text.KJV: 12:1 Ephraim feedeth on winde, and followeth after the East winde: hee daily increaseth lies and desolation, and they doe make a couenant with the Assyrians, and oyle is caried into Egypt.
text.NABRE: 12:1 Chapter 12 Infidelity of Israel - Ephraim has surrounded me with lies, the house of Israel, with deceit; Judah still wanders about with gods, and is faithful to holy ones.

text.NRSV: The Lord has an indictment against Judah, and will punish Jacob according to his ways, and repay him according to his deeds.
text.Rotherham: But, a controversy, hath Yahweh with Judah,—so that he may bring punishment on Jacob, according to his ways, According to his doings, repay him.
text.KJV: 12:2 The Lord hath also a controuersie with Iudah, and will punish Iacob according to his wayes, according to his doings will he recompense him.
text.NABRE: 12:2 Ephraim shepherds the wind, and pursues the east wind all day long. He multiplies lies and violence: They make a covenant with Assyria, and oil is carried to Egypt.

text.NRSV: In the womb he tried to supplant his brother, and in his manhood he strove with God.
text.Rotherham: In the womb, took he his brother by the heel; and, in his manly vigour, strove he with God:
text.KJV: 12:3 ¶ Hee tooke his brother by the heele in the wombe, and by his strength he had power with God.
text.NABRE: 12:3 The LORD has a dispute with Judah, and will punish Jacob for his conduct, and repay him for his deeds.

text.NRSV: He strove with the angel and prevailed, he wept and sought his favor; he met him at Bethel, and there he spoke with him.
text.Rotherham: Yea he strove against a Messenger, and prevailed, he wept, and made supplication unto him,—At Bethel, he found him, and, there, he spake with us;
text.KJV: 12:4 Yea, he had power ouer the Angel and preuailed: hee wept and made supplication vnto him: he found him in Bethel, and there he spake with vs.
text.NABRE: 12:4 In the womb he supplanted his brother, and in his vigor he contended with a divine being;

text.NRSV: The Lord the God of hosts, the Lord is his name!
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, is God of host,—Yahweh, is his memorial.
text.KJV: 12:5 Euen the Lord God of hosts, the Lord is his memoriall.
text.NABRE: 12:5 He contended with an angel and prevailed, he wept and entreated him. At Bethel he met with him, and there he spoke with him.

text.NRSV: But as for you, return to your God, hold fast to love and justice, and wait continually for your God.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, by thy God, shalt return,—lovingkindness and justice, do thou keep, so wait thou for thy God, continually.
text.KJV: 12:6 Therefore turne thou to thy God: keepe mercie and iudgement, and wait on thy God continually.
text.NABRE: 12:6 The LORD is the God of hosts, the LORD is his name!

text.NRSV: A trader, in whose hands are false balances, he loves to oppress.
text.Rotherham: A trafficker! in his hand, are balances of deceit, to oppress, he loveth.
text.KJV: 12:7 ¶ He is a merchant, the balances of deceit are in his hand: hee loueth to oppresse.
text.NABRE: 12:7 You must return to your God. Maintain loyalty and justice and always hope in your God.

text.NRSV: Ephraim has said, “Ah, I am rich, I have made a fortune for myself; in all my gain no offense has been found in me that would be sin.”
text.Rotherham: So then Ephraim said, Surely I have gotten me riches, I have found wealth for myself,—in all my toils, they cannot find in me perversity which is sin.
text.KJV: 12:8 And Ephraim said, Yet I am become rich, I haue found mee out substance: in all my labours they shall finde none iniquitie in mee, that were sinne.
text.NABRE: 12:8 A merchant who holds a false balance, he loves to extort!

text.NRSV: I am the Lord your God from the land of Egypt; I will make you live in tents again, as in the days of the appointed festival.
text.Rotherham: But, I, Yahweh, have been thy God, from the land of Egypt,—I will yet make thee dwell in tents, as in the days of appointed meeting.
text.KJV: 12:9 And I that am the Lord thy God from the lande of Egypt, will yet make thee to dwell in tabernacles, as in the dayes of the solemne feast.
text.NABRE: 12:9 Ephraim has said, “How rich I have become; I have made a fortune!” All his gain will not suffice for the guilt of his sin.

text.NRSV: I spoke to the prophets; it was I who multiplied visions, and through the prophets I will speak parables.
text.Rotherham: And I will lay my word upon the prophets, yea, I myself, have magnified, vision,—and, by the hand of the prophets, will I use similitudes.
text.KJV: 12:10 I haue also spoken by the prophets, and I haue multiplied visions, and vsed similitudes, by the ministerie of the prophets.
text.NABRE: 12:10 I the LORD have been your God, since the land of Egypt; I will again have you live in tents, as on feast days.

text.NRSV: In Gilead there is iniquity, they shall surely come to nothing. In Gilgal they sacrifice bulls, so their altars shall be like stone-heaps in the furrows of the field.
text.Rotherham: If, Gilead, is in sorrow, surely false, have they been, In Gilgal, have they sacrificed, bullocks,—their very altars, shall become as heaps upon the furrows of the field.
text.KJV: 12:11 Is there iniquitie in Gilead? surely they are vanitie, they sacrifice bullocks in Gilgal, yea their altars are as heapes in the furrowes of the fields.
text.NABRE: 12:11 I spoke to the prophets, I granted many visions, and through the prophets I told parables.

text.NRSV: Jacob fled to the land of Aram, there Israel served for a wife, and for a wife he guarded sheep.
text.Rotherham: When Jacob fled to the country of Syria, then Israel served for a wife, and, for a wife, he watched over a flock.
text.KJV: 12:12 And Iacob fled into the countrey of Syria, and Israel serued for a wife, and for a wife he kept sheepe.
text.NABRE: 12:12 In Gilead is falsehood, they have come to nothing; in Gilgal they sacrifice bulls, But their altars are like heaps of stones in the furrows of the field.

text.NRSV: By a prophet the Lord brought Israel up from Egypt, and by a prophet he was guarded.
text.Rotherham: And, by a prophet, Yahweh, brought up, Israel out of Egypt,—and, by a prophet, was he watched over.
text.KJV: 12:13 And by a Prophet the Lord brought Israel out of Egypt, and by a Prophet was he preserued.
text.NABRE: 12:13 Jacob fled to the land of Aram, and Israel served for a wife; for a wife he tended sheep.

text.NRSV: Ephraim has given bitter offense, so his Lord will leave his blood-guilt upon him, and will pay him back for his insults.
text.Rotherham: Ephraim hath provoked, very bitterly,—his own blood, therefore, upon him, will he leave, and, his reproach, shall his Lord, bring back to him.
text.KJV: 12:14 Ephraim prouoked him to anger, most bitterly: therefore shall he leaue his blood vpon him, and his reproch shall his Lord returne vnto him.
text.NABRE: 12:14 But by a prophet the LORD brought Israel out of Egypt, and by a prophet Israel was tended.

text.NRSV: When Ephraim spoke, there was trembling; he was exalted in Israel; but he incurred guilt through Baal and died.
text.Rotherham: When Ephraim, spake, there was terror, exalted was, he, in Israel,—but, when he became guilty with Baal, then he died.
text.KJV: 13:1 When Ephraim spake, trembling, he exalted himselfe in Israel, but, when he offended in Baal, he died.
text.NABRE: 13:1 Chapter 13 The Death of Ephraim - When Ephraim spoke there was terror; he was exalted in Israel; but he became guilty through Baal and died.

text.NRSV: And now they keep on sinning and make a cast image for themselves, idols of silver made according to their understanding, all of them the work of artisans. “Sacrifice to these,” they say. People are kissing calves!
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore they go on to sin, and have made them a Molten Thing out of their silver, after the notion of idols, the workmanship of craftsmen, all of it! Of them, are they saying—Ye sacrificers of men! The Great Calf, shall ye surely kiss!
text.KJV: 13:2 And now they sinne more and more, and haue made them molten images of their siluer, and idoles according to their owne vnderstanding, all of it the worke of the craftesmen: they say of them, Let the men that sacrifice, kisse the calues.
text.NABRE: 13:2 Now they continue to sin, making for themselves molten images, Silver idols according to their skill, all of them the work of artisans. “To these, offer sacrifice,” they say. People kiss calves!

text.NRSV: Therefore they shall be like the morning cloud or like the dew that goes away early, like chaff that swirls from the threshing floor or like smoke from a window.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, shall they become like the morning cloud, and like the dew early departing,—like chaff storm-driven out of the threshing-floor, and like smoke out of a chimney.
text.KJV: 13:3 Therefore they shalbe as the morning cloud, and as the early dew it passeth away, as the chaffe that is driuen with a whirlewinde out of the floore, and as the smoke out of the chimney.
text.NABRE: 13:3 Therefore, they will be like a morning cloud or like the dew that vanishes with the dawn, Like chaff storm-driven from the threshing floor or like smoke out of the window.

text.NRSV: Yet I have been the Lord your God ever since the land of Egypt; you know no God but me, and besides me there is no savior.
text.Rotherham: Yet, I, Yahweh, have been thy God from the land of Egypt,—and, god beside me, shalt thou not acknowledge, for, saviour, is there none besides me.
text.KJV: 13:4 Yet I am the Lord thy God from the land of Egypt, and thou shalt know no God, but me: for there is no sauiour beside me.
text.NABRE: 13:4 I, the LORD, am your God, since the land of Egypt; Gods apart from me you do not know; there is no savior but me.

text.NRSV: It was I who fed you in the wilderness, in the land of drought.
text.Rotherham: I, tended thee in the desert,—in a land parched with drought:
text.KJV: 13:5 ¶ I did know thee in the wildernesse, in the land of great drought.
text.NABRE: 13:5 I fed you in the wilderness, in the parched land.

text.NRSV: When I fed them, they were satisfied; they were satisfied, and their heart was proud; therefore they forgot me.
text.Rotherham: Whenever they were pastured, then were they satisfied, They were satisfied, and their heart, was lifted up,—because of this, they forgat me.
text.KJV: 13:6 According to their pasture, so were they filled: they were filled, and their heart was exalted: therefore haue they forgotten me.
text.NABRE: 13:6 When I fed them, they were satisfied; when satisfied, they became proud, therefore they forgot me.

text.NRSV: So I will become like a lion to them, like a leopard I will lurk beside the way.
text.Rotherham: Therefore am I become to them as a lion,—As a leopard by the way, do I watch.
text.KJV: 13:7 Therefore I will bee vnto them as a Lion, as a Leopard by the way will I obserue them.
text.NABRE: 13:7 So, I will be like a lion to them, like a leopard by the road I will keep watch.

text.NRSV: I will fall upon them like a bear robbed of her whelps, and will tear open the covering of their heart; there I will devour them like a lion, as a wild animal would mangle them.
text.Rotherham: I will fall upon them as a bear bereaved, and will read asunder the enclosure of their heart,—that I may devour them there, like a lioness, the wild beast of the field, shall tear them in pieces.
text.KJV: 13:8 I will meet them as a beare that is bereaued of her whelpes, and will rent the kall of their heart, and there will I deuoure them like a Lion: the wilde beast shall teare them.
text.NABRE: 13:8 I will attack them like a bear robbed of its young, and tear their hearts from their breasts; I will devour them on the spot like a lion, as a wild animal would rip them open.

text.NRSV: I will destroy you, O Israel; who can help you?
text.Rotherham: It hath utterly destroyed thee, O Israel, for it was against me, as thy helper!
text.KJV: 13:9 ¶ O Israel, thou hast destroied thy selfe, but in me is thine helpe.
text.NABRE: 13:9 I destroy you, Israel! who is there to help you?

text.NRSV: Where now is your king, that he may save you? Where in all your cities are your rulers, of whom you said, “Give me a king and princes”?
text.Rotherham: Where is thy king, then, that he may save thee throughout all thy cities? and thy judges, concerning whom thou saidst, Oh give me a king and rulers?
text.KJV: 13:10 I will be thy King: where is any other that may saue thee in all thy cities? and thy Iudges of whom thou saidst, Giue me a King and Princes?
text.NABRE: 13:10 Where now is your king, that he may rescue you? And all your princes, that they may defend you? Of whom you said, “Give me a king and princes”?

text.NRSV: I gave you a king in my anger, and I took him away in my wrath.
text.Rotherham: I might give thee a king in mine anger, and take him away in my wrath.
text.KJV: 13:11 I gaue thee a king in mine anger, and tooke him away in my wrath.
text.NABRE: 13:11 I give you a king in my anger, and I take him away in my wrath.

text.NRSV: Ephraim’s iniquity is bound up; his sin is kept in store.
text.Rotherham: Bound up, is the iniquity of Ephraim, stored away, his sin.
text.KJV: 13:12 The iniquitie of Ephraim is bound vp: his sinne is hid.
text.NABRE: 13:12 The guilt of Ephraim is wrapped up, his sin is stored away.

text.NRSV: The pangs of childbirth come for him, but he is an unwise son; for at the proper time he does not present himself at the mouth of the womb.
text.Rotherham: The pangs of a woman in labour, shall overtake him,—he, is a son, not wise, for, now, he cannot stand still, when children are about to be born.
text.KJV: 13:13 The sorrowes of a traueiling woman shall come vpon him, he is an vnwise sonne, for he should not stay long in the place of the breaking foorth of children.
text.NABRE: 13:13 The birth pangs will come for him, but this is an unwise child, Who, when it is time, does not present himself at the mouth of the womb.

text.NRSV: Shall I ransom them from the power of Sheol? Shall I redeem them from Death? O Death, where are your plagues? O Sheol, where is your destruction? Compassion is hidden from my eyes.
text.Rotherham: Out of the hand of hades, will I ransom them, out of death, will I redeem them,—Where is thy pestilence, O death? Where thy plague, O hades? Repentance, shall be hid from mine eyes.
text.KJV: 13:14 I will ransome them from the power of the graue: I will redeeme them from death: O death, I will be thy plagues, O graue, I will be thy destruction; repentance shall be hid from mine eyes.
text.NABRE: 13:14 Shall I deliver them from the power of Sheol? shall I redeem them from death? Where are your plagues, O death! where is your sting, Sheol! Compassion is hidden from my eyes.

text.NRSV: Although he may flourish among rushes, the east wind shall come, a blast from the Lord, rising from the wilderness; and his fountain shall dry up, his spring shall be parched. It shall strip his treasury of every precious thing.
text.Rotherham: Though, he, among brethren, be fruitful, there shall come in an east wind, the blast of Yahweh out of the desert coming up, that his spring, may dry up, and his fountain, be exhausted, he, will rob the treasure-house of all the vessels of delight.
text.KJV: 13:15 ¶ Though he be fruitfull among his brethren, an East winde shall come, the winde of the Lord shall come vp from the wildernesse, and his spring shall become drie, and his fountaine shalbe dried vp: he shall spoile the treasure of all pleasant vessels.
text.NABRE: 13:15 Though Ephraim may flourish among his brothers, an east wind will come, a wind from the LORD, rising from the wilderness, That will dry up his spring, and leave his fountain dry. It will loot his treasury of every precious thing.

text.NRSV: Samaria shall bear her guilt, because she has rebelled against her God; they shall fall by the sword, their little ones shall be dashed in pieces, and their pregnant women ripped open.
text.Rotherham: Samaria, shall be held guilty, for she hath rebelled against her God, By the sword, shall they fall, their infants, shall be dashed to the ground, and, his women with child, shall be ripped up.
text.KJV: 13:16 Samaria shall become desolate, for she hath rebelled against her God: they shall fall by the sword: their infants shalbe dashed in pieces, and their women with childe shalbe ript vp.

text.NRSV: Return, O Israel, to the Lord your God, for you have stumbled because of your iniquity.
text.Rotherham: Return thou, O Israel, unto Yahweh thy God,—for thou hast stumbled by thine iniquity.
text.KJV: 14:1 O Israel, returne vnto the Lord thy God; for thou hast fallen by thine iniquitie.
text.NABRE: 14:1 Chapter 14 - Samaria has become guilty, for she has rebelled against her God. They shall fall by the sword, their infants shall be dashed to pieces, their pregnant women shall be ripped open.

text.NRSV: Take words with you and return to the Lord; say to him, “Take away all guilt; accept that which is good, and we will offer the fruit of our lips.
text.Rotherham: Take with you words, and return to Yahweh: say unto him—Wholly, shalt thou take away iniquity, Accept, then, with favour, and we will make good the boldness of our lips!
text.KJV: 14:2 Take with you words, and turne to the Lord, say vnto him, Take away all iniquitie, and receiue vs graciously: so will wee render the calues of our lips.
text.NABRE: 14:2 Sincere Conversion and New Life - Return, Israel, to the LORD, your God; you have stumbled because of your iniquity.

text.NRSV: Assyria shall not save us; we will not ride on horses; we will say no more, ‘Our God,’ to the work of our hands. In you the orphan finds mercy.”
text.Rotherham: Assyria, shall not save us, Upon horses, will we not ride, neither will we say any more—Our god! to the work of our own hands! For, in thee, shall the fatherless, find compassion.
text.KJV: 14:3 Asshur shall not saue vs, we will not ride vpon horses, neither will wee say any more to the work of our hands, Yee are our gods: for in thee the fatherlesse findeth mercie.
text.NABRE: 14:3 Take with you words, and return to the LORD; Say to him, “Forgive all iniquity, and take what is good. Let us offer the fruit of our lips.

text.NRSV: I will heal their disloyalty; I will love them freely, for my anger has turned from them.
text.Rotherham: I will heal their apostacy, I will love them freely,—for mine anger, hath turned, from them.
text.KJV: 14:4 ¶ I will heale their backsliding, I will loue them freely: for mine anger is turned away from him.
text.NABRE: 14:4 Assyria will not save us, nor will we mount horses; We will never again say, ‘Our god,’ to the work of our hands; for in you the orphan finds compassion.”

text.NRSV: I will be like the dew to Israel; he shall blossom like the lily, he shall strike root like the forests of Lebanon.
text.Rotherham: I will become as the dew unto Israel, he shall break forth as the lily,—and he shall strike his roots as Lebanon:
text.KJV: 14:5 I wil be as the dew vnto Israel: hee shall grow as the lillie, and cast foorth his rootes as Lebanon.
text.NABRE: 14:5 I will heal their apostasy, I will love them freely; for my anger is turned away from them.

text.NRSV: His shoots shall spread out; his beauty shall be like the olive tree, and his fragrance like that of Lebanon.
text.Rotherham: His branches, shall spread, that, like an olive-tree, may be his fresh beauty,—and his fragrance, like Lebanon.
text.KJV: 14:6 His branches shall spread, and his beautie shalbe as the oliue tree, and his smell as Lebanon.
text.NABRE: 14:6 I will be like the dew for Israel: he will blossom like the lily; He will strike root like the Lebanon cedar,

text.NRSV: They shall again live beneath my shadow, they shall flourish as a garden; they shall blossom like the vine, their fragrance shall be like the wine of Lebanon.
text.Rotherham: They who dwell in his shade shall again show life like the corn, and break forth as the vine,—and, the remembrance of him, shall be like the wine of Lebanon.
text.KJV: 14:7 They that dwell vnder his shadow shall returne: they shall reuiue as the corne, & grow as the vine, the sent thereof shalbe as the wine of Lebanon.
text.NABRE: 14:7 and his shoots will go forth. His splendor will be like the olive tree and his fragrance like Lebanon cedar.

text.NRSV: O Ephraim, what have I to do with idols? It is I who answer and look after you. I am like an evergreen cypress; your faithfulness comes from me.
text.Rotherham: Ephraim [saith] —What to me any more are idols? I, have answered, and have closely observed him, I, am like a fir-tree that is green, From me, is thy fruit found.
text.KJV: 14:8 Ephraim shall say, What haue I to doe any more with idoles? I haue heard him, and obserued him: I am like a greene firre tree, from me is thy fruite found.
text.NABRE: 14:8 Again they will live in his shade; they will raise grain, They will blossom like the vine, and his renown will be like the wine of Lebanon.

text.NRSV: Those who are wise understand these things; those who are discerning know them. For the ways of the Lord are right, and the upright walk in them, but transgressors stumble in them.
text.Rotherham: Who is wise, that he may understand these things? intelligent, that he may take knowledge of them? For, straightforward, are the ways of Yahweh, and, the righteous, shall travel therein, but, transgressors, shall stumble therein.
text.KJV: 14:9 Who is wise, and hee shall vnderstand these things? prudent, and hee shall know them? for the wayes of the Lord are right, and the iust shall walke in them: but the transgressours shall fall therein.
text.NABRE: 14:9 Ephraim! What more have I to do with idols? I have humbled him, but I will take note of him. I am like a verdant cypress tree. From me fruit will be found for you!
